meta.name: Marathon Intermediate
meta.subtitle: Break 4 hours — 5:41/km for 42.2km
meta.description: For runners who have completed a marathon and want to break 4 hours. Twenty-four weeks of progressive volume with marathon-pace (MP) work woven into long runs from week 10 onwards.
meta.coach_notes: Sub-4 requires 5:41/km for 42.2km. The marathon-pace sections within Sunday long runs are the most specific training you will do. Running 5:41/km on tired legs in training is the closest thing to race day preparation available to you. Do not skip them and do not run them faster than prescribed.

weeks.title: Base week 1
weeks.note: Opening week. Establish your easy pace (5:55–6:20/km) and threshold feel. Nothing maximal.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:55–6:20/km — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

2km WU · 5x1km @ MP (5:41/km) (2.5min rec) · 2km CD — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

2km WU · 20min @ threshold (5:10–5:20/km) · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
10km easy · With strides — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
16km easy · 6:00–6:20/km — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 45min on runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30g+ protein, carbohydrates, vegetables.

weeks.title: Base week 2
weeks.note: Long run reaches 18km. MP sessions build race-pace familiarity.

sessions.det: 9km easy · 5:55–6:20/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 6x1km @ MP (2.5min rec) · 2km CD — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Back squat 3x10 · Romanian deadlift 3x10 · Bulgarian split squat 3x10 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.
2km WU · 22min @ threshold · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
10km easy · With strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
18km easy — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 45min on runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30g+ protein, carbohydrates, vegetables.

weeks.title: Peak block 1
weeks.note: 32km long run — 14km at MP. Your hardest long run.

sessions.det: 10km easy · 5:55–6:20/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · 8x1km @ MP (2min rec) · 2km CD — Peak-phase intervals — this is where your fitness is tested. These are hard but controlled. If the pace slips, the session is done.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
2km WU · 38min @ threshold · 2km CD — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30g+ protein, carbohydrates, vegetables.

weeks.title: Taper 3
weeks.note: Third taper week. Legs should feel increasingly good.

sessions.det: 5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
6km easy · 4x100m strides — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
8km easy — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 45min on runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30g+ protein, carbohydrates, vegetables.

weeks.title: Race week prep
weeks.note: Week before race week. Very easy.

sessions.det: 4km easy · strides — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
4km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
3km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
4km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 45min on runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30g+ protein, carbohydrates, vegetables.

weeks.title: Race week — sub-4 attempt
weeks.note: Race week. Everything you have done leads to Sunday.

sessions.det: 3km easy · 4x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
3km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Race: through 10km at 5:44/km, settle to 5:41, hold to 30km, build final 12km · Gel at 10/21/30/35km · Trust your MP training — Race day. Trust your training. Start conservatively — most races are won or lost in the second half.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey, or bagel with peanut butter. 600ml water. Only foods you have eaten before every long run.
400ml water. If you use caffeine, 200mg now.
10 minutes easy jog. Know your target splits by km. Start in the right corral.
Gel with a full cup of water. Never take a gel without water.
Gel with water at halfway. You should feel good here — if you feel bad, you started too fast.
Gel with water. This is where the race starts.
Last gel with water. Push now if you have anything left.
Isotonic drink immediately. Keep moving for 15 minutes. 30g protein within 30 minutes. Full meal within 2 hours.
